pattern: Since a match always grabs the closest available mirror to the left, keeping one stack of unmarked positions per letter lets every index resolve its pairing (or register itself for later) in constant time.
hints: The letter that would satisfy index i is fixed (the mirror of s[i]); what changes is whether an unmarked occurrence of that letter still exists to the left.
Keep 26 stacks, one per letter, holding the indices of that letter seen so far that haven't been marked yet.

At index i, check the stack for the mirror letter: if it has an entry, pop the most recent one, add the index gap to the score, and mark both as used; otherwise push i onto its own letter's stack to wait for a future match.

edge_inputs.description: no letter has its mirror anywhere in the string
single character can never be marked
three of the same mirrored letter leaves one unmatched
nested mirrors must pair with the closest partner, not the farthest
repeated mirrored pairs stack and unstack in order

stress.description: long random lowercase string exercising many mirror pairs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
